text.title: growth-product-hunt-launch Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for growth-product-hunt-launch. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Build an email list of 500+ interested users before launch. Without warm contacts, launch support is insufficient for early upvote velocity.
rule.rationale: Upvote velocity in the first hours determines the final ranking. Cold launches with no pre-built audience consistently fail to rank because they have no initial signal.

rule.statement: Secure a hunter with 1,000+ followers in the same niche. Hunter audience overlap with your product category matters more than raw follower count.
rule.rationale: A niche-relevant hunter's followers are more likely to upvote and comment because the product matches their interests. A large generic hunter provides less relevant traffic.

rule.statement: Do not launch if the product has major UX issues or is not functional. A PH launch surfaces all problems publicly and damages credibility permanently.
rule.rationale: PH comments are indexed and permanent. Negative launch comments about bugs or poor UX will follow the product indefinitely and reduce trust for future prospects.

rule.statement: Tagline must be under 60 characters, benefit-focused, and contain no jargon. Validate length programmatically after generation — agents frequently exceed the limit.
rule.rationale: The tagline is the primary hook for PH visitors scanning the feed. Over-limit taglines are truncated. Jargon excludes non-technical visitors who are valid early adopters.

rule.statement: Write the first comment before launch day. Have it ready to paste within 60 seconds of the post going live — early velocity is critical.
rule.rationale: First comments on PH posts receive the most visibility. A prepared maker comment with backstory, differentiators, and a special offer sets the tone for all subsequent engagement.

rule.statement: Tagline formula: [Action verb] [outcome] [for whom] [differentiator]. Under 60 characters. Validate length after generation.
rule.rationale: Action verbs signal what the product does immediately. Outcome communicates value. "For whom" targets the right audience. Under 60 characters prevents truncation in the feed.

rule.statement: Do not mention unfinished features in the listing or comments. PH commenters will test everything mentioned; negative comments about missing features compound quickly.
rule.rationale: PH comments are permanent and indexed. Overpromising on launch day creates immediate negative social proof that follows the product indefinitely.

rule.statement: Respond to EVERY comment within 1 hour. Never ask for upvotes directly — this is against PH TOS and looks desperate.
rule.rationale: Active maker engagement is a strong positive signal on PH. Accounts with high comment response rates rank better and are perceived as more trustworthy.
